Title: Influence of isopropylmalate synthase OsIPMS1 on seed vigour associated with amino acid and energy metabolism in rice

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: leucine biosynthesis / branched-chain amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses acetyl-CoA with 2-oxoisovalerate to form 2-isopropylmalate and CoA-SH as the committed step of leucine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed across developmental stages and tissues; increased in filling grain from 0 to 7 DAF then decreased after 14 DAF; strongly detected at 48 h imbibition when seeds begin germinating.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in various tissues and developmental stages; transcript strongly detected at 48 h imbibition in germinating seeds; relatively higher mRNA in 4–8 h priming seeds and reduced after 12 h priming.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Leucine; free amino acid pool associated with seed vigour
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports seed germination and seedling growth; associated with stress tolerance, GA biosynthesis, glycolysis/TCA-linked energy production, and seed vigour.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed germination/imbibition; seed priming; salt stress; drought stress (PEG); cold stress; direct seeding in soil; exogenous amino acid treatment; exogenous GA3 treatment; leucine feedback inhibition in enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IPMS enzyme with N-terminal catalytic region and C-terminal allosteric regulatory domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; CRISPR/Cas9 mutants; recombinant enzyme assay; leucine feedback assay; amino acid profiling; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; metabolite assays; GA3 rescue; amino acid rescue; seed germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes leucine biosynthesis and broader amino acid accumulation during germination, thereby enhancing GA biosynthesis, starch hydrolysis, glycolytic activity, ATP/energy levels, and ultimately seed vigour; loss of OsIPMS1 reduces these traits.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsIPMS1 encodes an isopropylmalate synthase that catalyses the committed step of leucine biosynthesis and is feedback-inhibited by leucine. 2. Disruption of OsIPMS1 in multiple mutant alleles caused lower germination potential/index, slower germination, weaker seedling growth, and poorer performance under salt, drought, cold, and direct-seeding conditions. 3. osipms1 mutants showed significant reductions in 11 amino acids during germination, including Leu, Ile, Val, Phe, Tyr, Thr, Cys, Ser, Gly, His and Pro. 4. Transcriptome and qRT-PCR data indicated reduced expression of glycolysis-related genes and lower pyruvate, acetyl-CoA, ATP, AMP and energy charge in mutants. 5. OsIPMS1 also promoted GA3 accumulation, expression of GA biosynthesis genes, amylase activities, glucose/fructose release, and could serve as a biomarker for optimal seed priming stop time.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper mainly functionally analyzes OsIPMS1, while OsIPMS2 is shown to have similar enzymatic and germination-related roles but is less deeply characterized. The terminal phenotype is seed vigour rather than a single nutrient trait; nutrient-metabolism causality is mediated through amino acid and energy metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes reduced seed vigour and poor emergence under stress/direct seeding; no explicit yield penalty of overexpression or natural high expression reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target/biomarker for improving seed vigour and optimizing seed priming stop time; potential editing or marker-assisted deployment should preserve OsIPMS1 function to support amino acid and energy metabolism during germination.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: leucine biosynthesis / branched-chain amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses acetyl-CoA with 2-oxoisovalerate to form 2-isopropylmalate and CoA-SH in leucine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed across developmental stages and tissues; increased in filling grain from 0 to 7 DAF then decreased after 14 DAF; strongly detected at 48 h imbibition.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Similar spatiotemporal expression pattern to OsIPMS1 in developing grains and germinating seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Leucine; free amino acid pool associated with seed vigour
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports seed germination and seedling growth through amino acid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed germination/imbibition; leucine feedback inhibition in enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IPMS enzyme with N-terminal catalytic region and C-terminal allosteric regulatory domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; leucine feedback assay; CRISPR/Cas9 mutant phenotyping during germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyses leucine biosynthesis and positively contributes to seed germination/seed vigour; mutant lines showed reduced germination speed and seedling growth under normal conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsIPMS2 encodes a second rice IPMS highly similar to OsIPMS1 and catalyses the committed step of leucine biosynthesis. 2. Its enzymatic activity is feedback-inhibited by leucine, although 2 mM leucine activated OsIPMS2 activity in one assay observation discussed by authors. 3. osipms2 mutants showed significant decreases in germination speed and seedling growth, supporting a similar role to OsIPMS1 in seed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsIPMS2 was not the main mechanistic focus of downstream metabolite/transcript analyses. The paper states OsIPMS1 and OsIPMS2 may have similar but not identical biochemical characteristics.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces germination speed and seedling growth.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive target for improving seed vigour, though the study emphasizes OsIPMS1 as the more practical biomarker.
